strap: Cambridgeshire & Peterborough Engineering Biology draws £267m of UKRI lead-led funding across 424 grants, anchored by Cambridge (18%), European Bioinformatics Institute (9%), with Microsoft Research on the industrial side. 4 Companies House-traced spin-outs translate to £67m UKRI per spin-out.
behaviour: The cluster shows medium-confidence "Coordinating instead of deciding" and "Re-proving instead of narrowing" behaviour — multi-actor coordination distributes risk across institutional partners without forcing the strategic option-collapse that would convert capability into a defined pathway.
